mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly increasing present value of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenues and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.5pp threshold over a 3-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery, which amplifies earnings growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which improves short-term corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during early-cycle expansion phases across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly increases above 0.4% are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in > 70% of cases over a rolling 3-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with stable or declining unemployment confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high PAYEMS growth and low UNRATE change fails to yield positive 20-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding aggregate economic activity, which directly increases corporate revenue streams and supports positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns (t-stat < 1.5) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month drop in housing starts signals a contraction in the construction sector and broader consumer confidence, which reduces near-term economic activity and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST declines exceeding 5% are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of observed instances during housing correction periods.
